content_en:
Obedience is only with respect to what is good and acceptable (maroof).”

(2) Remaining in the House and Not Going out except with the Husband’s Permission

Allah says in the Quran, Fan CCE a en aa ee a I Seed HEY SS Bos, “And stay in your houses and do not display yourselves like that during the times of Ignorance” (al-Ahzaab 33). In this verse, Allah ordered the

wives of the Prophet (peace be upon him), and the women of this Nation also follow them in this order, to remain in their houses as a protection

' Recorded by ibn Hibbaan in his Sabeeb (#4163), The Book on Marriage: Mention of the prescri ing of Paradise for the woman when she obeys her husband as well as fulfills the obligatory duties toward Allah. [This hadith has been graded sahib by both al-Albaani and Shuaib al-Arnaoot. See al-Albaani, Sabeeb al-Jaami al-Sagheer, vol. 1, p. 174; Ameer al-Deen al-Faarisi, Al-Jbsaan fi Tagreeb Saheeb ibn Hibbaan, Shuaib al-Amaoot, ed. (Beirut: Muassasat al-Risaalah, 1991), vol. 9, p. 471—JzZ]

Recorded. by al-Bukhari (#4340), Book on Military Expeditions, Chapter on the expedition of Abdullah ibn Hudhaafah al-Sahmi and Algamah ibn Muharriz alMudlaji; Muslim (#1840) Book on Governmental Authority, Chapter on the obligation to obey the ruler on matters that are not acts of disobedience and its prohibition on matters that are acts of disobedience.

‘The Figh of Marriage

for them and a way of guarding the rights of their husbands. They are not to leave their houses except due to true needs.! Shaikh al-Islam ibn Taimiyah stated,

It is not allowed for a wife to leave her house except by the permission of her husband. It is not allowed for anyone to take her from him or to prevent her from going to him, even if she be a nursing mother, midwife or of any other type of occupation. If she leaves the house of her husband without his permission, she is being recalcitrant (naashizah), disobedient to Allah and His Messenger and deserving of punishment.”

(3) Responding to His Call When He Calls Her to His Bed

This was discussed earlier.

(4) Not Allowing Anyone to Enter His House except with His Permission

It is not permissible for the wife to allow someone to enter her husband’s house if that is displeasing to the husband. The Prophet (peace be upon him) said,

se seb ee fabystet a hfe ae be

The preferred interpretation is that permission should not be given to anyone to enter your houses and sit on your

' See Tafseer ibn Katheer, vol 3 Majmoo al-Fatawaa, vol. 32, :

Recorded by Muslim in his (#1218), The Book on the Pilgrimage, Chapter on the pilgrimage of the Prophet (peace be upon him); it is part of the farewell speech of the Prophet (peace be uponthim),

The Figh of Marriage

furnishings whom he [the husband] dislikes. This is regardless if the one given permission is a non-related man, a woman or one of the wife’s relatives. The prohibition extends to all of them. The wife is not to permit any man, woman, relative or otherwise [to enter into her husband’s house] unless she knows or believes that the husband does not dislike that person [to enter].

Sahih Muslim also contains the hadith of Abu Hurairah who narrated that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said,

Lond ee ee ne Po 5b YU Say a GOS “(The wife] is not to allow anyone into his house while he is present except with his permission.”* In al-Fath, ibn Hajr made the following

The condition, “while he is present,” is not meant to imply anything to the contrary but is simply stated as what is the normal case. In other words, simply because the husband is not present does not mean that the wife is now allowed to permit anyone she wishes to enter his house. In fact, the prohibition in that case is even more stressed based on other confirmed hadith with prohibit her from allowing those to enter while he is not present.?

Al-Imam al-Nawawi wrote that this indicates that she cannot usurp the authority of the husband by permitting anyone to enter into his house except with his permission. This is applied to those cases where the wife does not know of the husband’s non-objection to a specific person. If she knows that her husband is pleased with a person, there is no harm upon her [if she allows that person to enter his house], such as when it was the custom of the husband to allow his guests to enter a

3 See Sharh sahih Muslim.

sahih Muslim (#1026), the Book of zakah, Chapter on what a slave can spend from the wealth of his master,

Fath al-Baari, vol. 9, p. 296.

The Figh of Marriage

specific part of the house, regardless of whether he is present or absent. In that case, she is not in need of specific permission from the husband to allow them to enter.

(5) Serving the Husband

From the rights of the husband upon the wife is that she must serve him in such matters as bringing up the children, preparing his food, taking care of the clothes and so forth. This is proper as a result of his being in charge of the affairs and it is one of the goals of a sound marriage. In this way, the structural make-up of the household and the affairs of the household will be according to what is natural. The wife should neither disdain nor be too proud to serve her husband if she has the ability to do so and he wants that from her.

In the Sahihs of al-Bukhari and Muslim it states that Faatimah, the daughter of the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him), went to the Prophet (peace be upon him) to complain about the harm her hand was receiving from the stone handmill. She had heard that the Prophet (peace be upon him) had just received some slaves. However, she did not find the Prophet (peace be upon him), so she told Aishah about the matter and when the Prophet (peace be upon him) came, Aishah informed him about Fatima’s request. Ali ibn Abu Taalib said, “So he came to us when we had gone to bed. We were going to get up for him but he said, ‘Stay in your places.’ He came and sat between us and I could feel the coldness of his feet upon my stomach. He said, ‘Shall I not guide you to something that is better than what you have asked for? When you go to your beds, extol Allah’s perfection thirty-three times [by saying subhaanallahj, praise Allah thirty-three times and extol Allah’s greatness thirty-four times. That is better for you than a servant.’ I never abandoned that practice afterwards.” He was asked, “Not even on the night of the Battle of Siffeen?” He replied, “Not even on the night of the Battle of Siffeen.”!

| Sabib al-Bukbari in a number of places, including (#5361), the Book on

Expenditures, Chapter on the wife working in the house of her husband; Sabib Muslim (#2182), Book on Greeting, Chapter on the permissibility of giving a lift to. a

The Figh of Marriage

It has been authentically narrated from Asmaa bint Abu Bakr alSiddeeq that she said, “I used to serve al-Zubair ibn al-Awwaam— her husband— concerning all of the matters within the house. He used to hhave a horse that I used to take care of and give it its fodder. I would feed the horse and draw water for it.” She would also sew the waterbucket and prepare the dough. She used to carry the dates upon her head from [al-Zubair’s] land which was about two miles away from her house.!

Shaikh al-Islam ibn Taimiyah said:

The scholars have disputed over whether or not it is obligatory upon her [the wife} to serve him [the husband] in things like taking care of the household items, serving the food, drink, bread, flour, food for his slaves and his animals, such as preparing fodder for his horse and so on. Some say that such service is not obligatory. That opinion is weak, like the weakness of those who say that it is not obligatory upon him to have intimacy and sexual relations with her. This [not serving the husbands] means she is not treating him in a good manner. In fact, the companion on a journey, who is similar to a person and his mate ina household, if he does not help in doing what is needed has not treated his companion in the proper way.

It is said— and it is correct— that such service is obligatory. The husband is described as her master [or lord, sayyid] in the Book of Allah.? She is like his captive as described in the sunnah of the Messenger of Allah

non-related woman if she is exhausted on the road. Siffeen is a place on the banks of the Euphrates wherein took place the famous battle between Ali and Muawiyah (may allah be pleased with both of them) in the year 37 A.H. (Mujam al-Buldaan, vol. 3,

14). Ps See Sabib alser (#5224), Sabib Muslim (#2182), Musnad al-Imaam

He is relerring, 4 the verse, they both found her master at the door" (Yoosuf 25).

‘The Figh of Marriage

(peace be upon him).! It is obligatory upon the captive and the slave to serve. That is what is well-known and accepted. Then there are some who say that light service is what is obligatory upon her while others say that the service that is customary and accepted is what is obligatory upon her. [The latter opinion] is the correct opinion. She must serve her husband according to what is customary for someone like her with respect to someone like him. The situation differs under different circumstances. The serving by the bedouin woman is not like that by the city woman. The serving by the strong woman is not like that by a weak woman.?

Those who say that her serving [her husband] is obligatory cite as an argument the fact that such was the customary practice among those whom Allah addressed with His speech. Furthermore, the dower is in exchange for the right to sexual intercourse. But each spouse fulfills his [or her] desire with the other. Therefore, Allah has obliged the man to maintain her, clothe her and give her housing in exchange for being able to enjoy her and for her serving him. And this has been the normal custom among the spouses. And again, the contracts that are left
content_id:
Ketaatan hanyalah dalam hal-hal yang baik dan dapat diterima (maroof)."

(2) Tinggal di Rumah dan Tidak Keluar Kecuali dengan Izin Suami

Allah berfirman dalam Quran, Fan CCE a en aa ee a I Seed HEY SS Bos, "Dan hendaklah kamu tetap di rumahmu dan janganlah kamu berhias dan bertingkah laku seperti orang-orang Jahiliyah dahulu" (al-Ahzaab 33). Dalam ayat ini, Allah memerintahkan

istri-istri Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam), dan para wanita umat ini pun mengikuti mereka dalam perintah ini, untuk tetap tinggal di rumah mereka sebagai perlindungan

' Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Hibbaan dalam Saheeh-nya (#4163), Kitab Nikah: Penyebutan tentang disyariatkannya surga bagi wanita yang menaati suaminya serta memenuhi kewajiban-kewajiban kepada Allah. [Hadith ini telah dinilai sahih oleh al-Albaani dan Shuaib al-Arnaoot. Lihat al-Albaani, Saheeh al-Jaami al-Sagheer, jil. 1, hal. 174; Ameer al-Deen al-Faarisi, Al-Ihsaan fi Taqreeb Saheeb ibn Hibbaan, Shuaib al-Arnaoot, ed. (Beirut: Muassasat al-Risaalah, 1991), jil. 9, hal. 471—JzZ]

Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari (#4340), Kitab Peperangan, Bab tentang ekspedisi Abdullah ibn Hudhaafah al-Sahmi dan Algamah ibn Muharriz al-Mudlaji; Muslim (#1840) Kitab Kepemimpinan, Bab tentang kewajiban menaati pemimpin dalam hal-hal yang bukan kemaksiatan dan larangan menaatinya dalam hal kemaksiatan.

'Fiqh Pernikahan

bagi mereka dan sebagai cara menjaga hak-hak suami mereka. Mereka tidak boleh keluar dari rumah mereka kecuali karena kebutuhan yang nyata.! Syaikh al-Islam Ibnu Taimiyah menyatakan,

Tidak diperbolehkan bagi seorang istri untuk keluar dari rumahnya kecuali dengan izin suaminya. Tidak diperbolehkan bagi siapapun untuk membawanya pergi darinya atau mencegahnya untuk kembali kepadanya, meskipun ia adalah seorang ibu yang menyusui, bidan, atau profesi lainnya. Jika ia keluar dari rumah suaminya tanpa seizinnya, ia telah berbuat nusyuz, bermaksiat kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya, dan berhak mendapat hukuman."

(3) Memenuhi Panggilannya Ketika Ia Memanggilnya ke Tempat Tidurnya

Hal ini telah dibahas sebelumnya.

(4) Tidak Mengizinkan Siapapun Masuk ke Rumahnya Kecuali dengan Izinnya

Tidak diperbolehkan bagi istri untuk mengizinkan seseorang masuk ke rumah suaminya jika hal itu tidak disukai oleh suami. Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda,

se seb ee fabystet a hfe ae be

Penafsiran yang lebih tepat adalah bahwa izin tidak boleh diberikan kepada siapapun untuk masuk ke rumah kalian dan duduk di atas

' Lihat Tafseer Ibnu Katsir, jil. 3; Majmoo al-Fataawa, jil. 32, :

Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam kitabnya (#1218), Kitab Haji, Bab tentang haji Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam); ini merupakan bagian dari khutbah perpisahan Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam),

Fiqh Pernikahan

perabotan rumah orang yang tidak disukainya [suami]. Hal ini berlaku tanpa memandang apakah orang yang diberi izin itu adalah laki-laki asing, seorang wanita, atau salah satu kerabat istri. Larangan ini berlaku untuk semua mereka. Istri tidak boleh mengizinkan siapapun, baik laki-laki, perempuan, kerabat, maupun lainnya [masuk ke rumah suaminya] kecuali jika ia mengetahui atau meyakini bahwa suami tidak membenci orang tersebut [untuk masuk].

Sahih Muslim juga memuat hadith Abu Hurairah yang meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda,

Lond ee ee ne Po 5b YU Say a GOS "[Istri] tidak boleh mengizinkan siapapun masuk ke rumahnya saat ia hadir kecuali dengan seizinnya."* Dalam al-Fath, Ibnu Hajar membuat komentar berikut

Syarat "saat ia hadir" tidak dimaksudkan untuk menyiratkan sebaliknya, melainkan hanya disebutkan sebagai keadaan yang lazim terjadi. Dengan kata lain, hanya karena suami tidak hadir bukan berarti istri sekarang diperbolehkan untuk mengizinkan siapapun yang ia kehendaki untuk masuk ke rumahnya. Bahkan, larangan dalam kasus itu lebih ditekankan berdasarkan hadith-hadith sahih lainnya yang melarangnya mengizinkan orang-orang masuk ketika ia tidak hadir.?

Al-Imam al-Nawawi menulis bahwa hal ini menunjukkan bahwa istri tidak dapat merampas wewenang suami dengan mengizinkan siapapun masuk ke rumahnya kecuali dengan seizinnya. Hal ini berlaku dalam kasus-kasus di mana istri tidak mengetahui ketidakberatan suami terhadap orang tertentu. Jika ia mengetahui bahwa suaminya senang dengan seseorang, maka tidak ada dosa baginya [jika ia mengizinkan orang tersebut masuk ke rumahnya], seperti ketika sudah menjadi kebiasaan suami untuk mengizinkan tamu-tamunya masuk ke

3 Lihat Syarh Sahih Muslim.

Sahih Muslim (#1026), Kitab Zakat, Bab tentang apa yang boleh dibelanjakan seorang budak dari harta tuannya,

Fath al-Baari, jil. 9, hal. 296.

Fiqh Pernikahan

bagian tertentu dari rumah, baik ia hadir maupun tidak hadir. Dalam kasus tersebut, istri tidak memerlukan izin khusus dari suami untuk mengizinkan mereka masuk.

(5) Melayani Suami

Di antara hak-hak suami atas istri adalah bahwa ia harus melayaninya dalam hal-hal seperti mengurus anak-anak, menyiapkan makanannya, merawat pakaian, dan sebagainya. Hal ini adalah sesuatu yang wajar sebagai akibat dari kedudukannya sebagai penanggung jawab urusan rumah tangga dan merupakan salah satu tujuan dari pernikahan yang baik. Dengan cara ini, susunan struktur rumah tangga dan urusan-urusan rumah tangga akan berjalan sesuai dengan fitrah. Istri tidak seharusnya meremehkan atau terlalu bangga untuk melayani suaminya jika ia mampu melakukannya dan suami menginginkan hal tersebut darinya.

Dalam Sahih al-Bukhari dan Muslim disebutkan bahwa Faatimah, putri Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam), pergi menemui Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) untuk mengadukan penderitaan yang dialami tangannya akibat batu penggilingan. Ia telah mendengar bahwa Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) baru saja menerima beberapa budak. Namun, ia tidak menemukan Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam), maka ia menceritakan masalahnya kepada Aishah, dan ketika Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) datang, Aishah memberitahunya tentang permintaan Fatimah. Ali ibn Abu Taalib berkata, "Maka ia datang kepada kami ketika kami telah pergi tidur. Kami hendak bangkit untuknya tetapi ia berkata, 'Tetaplah di tempat kalian.' Ia datang dan duduk di antara kami dan aku bisa merasakan dinginnya kakinya di perutku. Ia berkata, 'Maukah aku tunjukkan kepada kalian sesuatu yang lebih baik dari apa yang kalian minta? Ketika kalian pergi tidur, bertasbihlah kepada Allah tiga puluh tiga kali [dengan mengucapkan subhaanallah], pujilah Allah tiga puluh tiga kali, dan agungkanlah Allah tiga puluh empat kali. Itu lebih baik bagi kalian daripada seorang pembantu.' Aku tidak pernah meninggalkan amalan itu setelahnya." Ia ditanya, "Bahkan pada malam Pertempuran Siffeen?" Ia menjawab, "Bahkan pada malam Pertempuran Siffeen."!

| Sahih al-Bukhari di beberapa tempat, termasuk (#5361), Kitab Nafkah, Bab tentang istri yang bekerja di rumah suaminya; Sahih Muslim (#2182), Kitab Salam, Bab tentang kebolehan memberi tumpangan kepada

Fiqh Pernikahan

Telah diriwayatkan secara sahih dari Asmaa binti Abu Bakr al-Siddeeq bahwa ia berkata, "Aku biasa melayani al-Zubair ibn al-Awwaam— suaminya— dalam semua urusan di dalam rumah. Ia biasa memiliki seekor kuda yang aku rawat dan aku beri pakannya. Aku memberi makan kuda itu dan mengambilkan air untuknya." Ia juga menjahit ember air dan mempersiapkan adonan. Ia biasa membawa kurma di atas kepalanya dari tanah [al-Zubair] yang berjarak sekitar dua mil dari rumahnya.!

Syaikh al-Islam Ibnu Taimiyah berkata:

Para ulama berbeda pendapat tentang apakah wajib bagi istri untuk melayani suami dalam hal-hal seperti merawat perabotan rumah tangga, menyajikan makanan, minuman, roti, tepung, makanan untuk para budaknya dan hewan-hewannya, seperti menyiapkan pakan untuk kudanya dan sebagainya. Sebagian berpendapat bahwa pelayanan seperti itu tidak wajib. Pendapat itu lemah, seperti lemahnya pendapat mereka yang mengatakan bahwa tidak wajib baginya untuk berhubungan intim dan berjima' dengannya. Hal ini [tidak melayani suami] berarti ia tidak memperlakukannya dengan cara yang baik. Bahkan, teman seperjalanan, yang serupa dengan seseorang dan pasangannya dalam sebuah rumah tangga, jika ia tidak membantu dalam melakukan apa yang diperlukan, maka ia tidak memperlakukan temannya dengan cara yang semestinya.

Ada yang berpendapat— dan pendapat inilah yang benar— bahwa pelayanan seperti itu adalah wajib. Suami disebut sebagai tuannya [atau penguasanya, sayyid] dalam Kitab Allah.? Ia seperti tawanannya sebagaimana dijelaskan dalam sunnah Rasulullah

wanita yang bukan mahram jika ia kelelahan di jalan. Siffeen adalah sebuah tempat di tepi sungai Eufrat di mana terjadi pertempuran terkenal antara Ali dan Muawiyah (semoga Allah meridhai keduanya) pada tahun 37 H. (Mu'jam al-Buldaan, jil. 3,

14). Ps Lihat Sahih al-Bukhari (#5224), Sahih Muslim (#2182), Musnad al-Imaam

Beliau merujuk kepada ayat, "mereka berdua mendapati tuannya di depan pintu" (Yoosuf 25).

Fiqh Pernikahan

(shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam). Adalah wajib bagi tawanan dan budak untuk melayani. Itulah yang telah dikenal dan diterima. Kemudian ada sebagian yang berpendapat bahwa pelayanan ringan adalah yang wajib atasnya, sementara yang lain berpendapat bahwa pelayanan yang lazim dan diterima adalah yang wajib atasnya. [Pendapat yang terakhir] adalah pendapat yang benar. Ia harus melayani suaminya sesuai dengan apa yang lazim bagi seseorang seperti dirinya terhadap seseorang seperti suaminya. Keadaan berbeda dalam situasi yang berbeda. Pelayanan wanita badui tidaklah sama dengan pelayanan wanita kota. Pelayanan wanita yang kuat tidaklah sama dengan pelayanan wanita yang lemah.

Mereka yang berpendapat bahwa pelayanannya [kepada suami] adalah wajib berdalil dengan kenyataan bahwa hal itu merupakan kebiasaan yang berlaku di kalangan mereka yang Allah firmankan kepada mereka. Lebih lanjut, mahar adalah imbalan atas hak hubungan suami istri. Namun setiap pasangan memenuhi hasratnya dengan yang lain. Oleh karena itu, Allah mewajibkan suami untuk menafkahinya, memberinya pakaian dan tempat tinggal sebagai imbalan atas kemampuannya menikmatinya dan atas pelayanannya kepadanya. Dan inilah kebiasaan yang berlaku di antara pasangan suami istri. Dan sekali lagi, akad-akad yang dibiarkan

(2) Remaining in the House and Not Going out except with the Husband’s Permission

الله says in the القرآن الكريم, Fan CCE a en aa ee a I Seed HEY SS Bos, “And stay in your houses and do not display yourselves like that during the times of Ignorance” (al-Ahzaab 33). In this verse, الله ordered the

wives of النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم), and the women of this Nation also follow them in this order, to remain in their houses as a protection

‘The Figh of Marriage

for them and a way of guarding the rights of their husbands. They are not to leave their houses except due to true needs.! Shaikh al-Islam ibn Taimiyah stated,

It is not allowed for a wife to leave her house except by the permission of her husband. It is not allowed for anyone to take her from him or to prevent her from going to him, even if she be a nursing mother, midwife or of any other type of occupation. If she leaves the house of her husband without his permission, she is being recalcitrant (naashizah), disobedient to الله and His Messenger and deserving of punishment.”

(3) Responding to His Call When He Calls Her to His Bed

This was discussed earlier.

(4) Not Allowing Anyone to Enter His House except with His Permission

It is not permissible for the wife to allow someone to enter her husband’s house if that is displeasing to the husband. النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) said,

se seb ee fabystet a hfe ae be

The preferred interpretation is that permission should not be given to anyone to enter your houses and sit on your

The Figh of Marriage

furnishings whom he [the husband] dislikes. This is regardless if the one given permission is a non-related man, a woman or one of the wife’s relatives. The prohibition extends to all of them. The wife is not to permit any man, woman, relative or otherwise [to enter into her husband’s house] unless she knows or believes that the husband does not dislike that person [to enter].

Sahih Muslim also contains the الحديث of Abu Hurairah who narrated that رسول الله ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) said,

Lond ee ee ne Po 5b YU Say a GOS “(The wife] is not to allow anyone into his house while he is present except with his permission.”* In al-Fath, ibn Hajr made the following

The condition, “while he is present,” is not meant to imply anything to the contrary but is simply stated as what is the normal case. In other words, simply because the husband is not present does not mean that the wife is now allowed to permit anyone she wishes to enter his house. In fact, the prohibition in that case is even more stressed based on other confirmed الحديث with prohibit her from allowing those to enter while he is not present.?

Al-Imam al-Nawawi wrote that this indicates that she cannot usurp the authority of the husband by permitting anyone to enter into his house except with his permission. This is applied to those cases where the wife does not know of the husband’s non-objection to a specific person. If she knows that her husband is pleased with a person, there is no harm upon her [if she allows that person to enter his house], such as when it was the custom of the husband to allow his guests to enter a

3 See Sharh sahih Muslim.

sahih Muslim (#1026), the Book of zakah, Chapter on what a slave can spend from the wealth of his master,

Fath al-Baari, vol. 9, p. 296.

The Figh of Marriage

specific part of the house, regardless of whether he is present or absent. In that case, she is not in need of specific permission from the husband to allow them to enter.

(5) Serving the Husband

From the rights of the husband upon the wife is that she must serve him in such matters as bringing up the children, preparing his food, taking care of the clothes and so forth. This is proper as a result of his being in charge of the affairs and it is one of the goals of a sound marriage. In this way, the structural make-up of the household and the affairs of the household will be according to what is natural. The wife should neither disdain nor be too proud to serve her husband if she has the ability to do so and he wants that from her.

In the Sahihs of al-Bukhari and Muslim it states that Faatimah, the daughter of رسول الله ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم), went to النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) to complain about the harm her hand was receiving from the stone handmill. She had heard that النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) had just received some slaves. However, she did not find النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم), so she told Aishah about the matter and when النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) came, Aishah informed him about Fatima’s request. Ali ibn Abu Taalib said, “So he came to us when we had gone to bed. We were going to get up for him but he said, ‘Stay in your places.’ He came and sat between us and I could feel the coldness of his feet upon my stomach. He said, ‘Shall I not guide you to something that is better than what you have asked for? When you go to your beds, extol الله’s perfection thirty-three times [by saying subhaanallahj, praise الله thirty-three times and extol الله’s greatness thirty-four times. That is better for you than a servant.’ I never abandoned that practice afterwards.” He was asked, “Not even on the night of the Battle of Siffeen?” He replied, “Not even on the night of the Battle of Siffeen.”!

| Sabib al-Bukbari in a number of places, including (#5361), the Book on

Expenditures, Chapter on the wife working in the house of her husband; Sabib Muslim (#2182), Book on Greeting, Chapter on the permissibility of giving a lift to. a

The Figh of Marriage

It has been authentically narrated from Asmaa bint Abu Bakr alSiddeeq that she said, “I used to serve al-Zubair ibn al-Awwaam— her husband— concerning all of the matters within the house. He used to hhave a horse that I used to take care of and give it its fodder. I would feed the horse and draw water for it.” She would also sew the waterbucket and prepare the dough. She used to carry the dates upon her head from [al-Zubair’s] land which was about two miles away from her house.!

Shaikh al-Islam ibn Taimiyah said:

The scholars have disputed over whether or not it is obligatory upon her [the wife} to serve him [the husband] in things like taking care of the household items, serving the food, drink, bread, flour, food for his slaves and his animals, such as preparing fodder for his horse and so on. Some say that such service is not obligatory. That opinion is weak, like the weakness of those who say that it is not obligatory upon him to have intimacy and sexual relations with her. This [not serving the husbands] means she is not treating him in a good manner. In fact, the companion on a journey, who is similar to a person and his mate ina household, if he does not help in doing what is needed has not treated his companion in the proper way.

It is said— and it is correct— that such service is obligatory. The husband is described as her master [or lord, sayyid] in the Book of الله.? She is like his captive as described in the السنة النبوية of رسول الله ﷺ

non-related woman if she is exhausted on the road. Siffeen is a place on the banks of the Euphrates wherein took place the famous battle between Ali and Muawiyah (may الله be pleased with both of them) in the year 37 A.H. (Mujam al-Buldaan, vol. 3,

14). Ps See Sabib alser (#5224), Sabib Muslim (#2182), Musnad al-Imaam

He is relerring, 4 the verse, they both found her master at the door" (Yoosuf 25).

‘The Figh of Marriage

(صلى الله عليه وسلم).! It is obligatory upon the captive and the slave to serve. That is what is well-known and accepted. Then there are some who say that light service is what is obligatory upon her while others say that the service that is customary and accepted is what is obligatory upon her. [The latter opinion] is the correct opinion. She must serve her husband according to what is customary for someone like her with respect to someone like him. The situation differs under different circumstances. The serving by the bedouin woman is not like that by the city woman. The serving by the strong woman is not like that by a weak woman.?

Those who say that her serving [her husband] is obligatory cite as an argument the fact that such was the customary practice among those whom الله addressed with His speech. Furthermore, the dower is in exchange for the right to sexual intercourse. But each spouse fulfills his [or her] desire with the other. Therefore, الله has obliged the man to maintain her, clothe her and give her housing in exchange for being able to enjoy her and for her serving him. And this has been the normal custom among the spouses. And again, the contracts that are left
